label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage and find the correct answer. First, the question is about Yves Gomes and which statement is true. Looking at the passage: (Yves Gomes is now a university student in Maryland, but he may soon have to leave the country. "I still want to continue with my college education and I want to be able to study here and go to medical school," he said.) So he is in the U.S., wants to stay to study and go to medical school. Next: (Gomes is one of thousands of young illegal immigrants who grew up in the United States and are now studying at American colleges and universities. He is doing well in his studies, but he is fighting deportation to India, a country he left with his parents when he was just 14 months old. They were deported more than a year ago.) So he left India at 14 months (very young) with his parents, but they were deported more than a year ago. So he's facing deportation to India now, which is a country he doesn't know well, having grown up in the U.S. Let's look at the options: Option (A): He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. But the passage says he left India when he was 14 months old. That suggests he was born in the U.S. or that his family left India and came to the U.S., but the passage says he was born in the U.S. because it says he's an "illegal immigrant who grew up in the United States." So this statement is false. Option (B): His parents were forced to leave India more than a year ago. The passage says: "They were deported more than a year ago." So "they" refers to his parents. Being deported means they were forced to leave the U.S., not India. The passage says they were deported (from the U.S.) to India. So this statement is confusing the direction. They left the U.S. and went back to India. The statement says they were forced to leave India, which is the opposite of what the passage says. So this is false. Option (C): He wants to finish school in America and become a nurse in India after graduation. The passage says he wants to finish school and become a doctor. Also, he wants to stay in the U.S., not go to India. So this is false. Option (D): He is optimistic about the passage of the DREAMAct.
